name: Team Ryan
subject: American jobs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
****
Friend --

Despite all the drama and distractions on the news, your House Republicans are focused on solving the real problems you care about -- especially when it comes to jobs and paychecks.

**Already, we 've saved billions of dollars by eliminating regulations, passed reforms to improve job training, and we replaced the Dodd-Frank law with real relief for small businesses and community banks. **

You may not always hear about these things in the news, [**but they are happening.**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MzAxMzUzNTMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMTE5ODEwNgS2&mt=1&rt=0) Your House Republicans are continuing to fight for pro-employee, pro-business, pro-job policies.

But news like this just isn't getting out. [**Help us spread the word >>**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MzAxMzUzNTMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMTE5ODEwNgS2&mt=1&rt=0)

**The fact is,** **your Republican Representatives are still fighting for the agenda you voted for in 2016, even though the news doesn't always cover our important work.**

We're working for you, Friend.**[If you're fed up with the news coverage and want to get our message out, take a stand today.](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MzAxMzUzNTMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMTE5ODEwNgS2&mt=1&rt=0)**

Thank you,

name: Debbie
subject: Planned Parenthood
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| All of the Republicans' health care plans in Congress -- including the 'skinny repeal' \-- had one provision in common: **they all defunded Planned Parenthood.** Even though the 'skinny repeal' was defeated, it's obvious that eliminating women's health care options is still a top priority for Republicans. We can't expect the GOP to leave Planned Parenthood alone for good. **That 's why we need to keep standing up for women's health and Planned Parenthood. We are entitled to affordable, accessible care -- including reproductive care.** [_**Add your name to protect Planned Parenthood.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e26/622b7b5b/51c4119/307bdd88/3507139711/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e170805") Planned Parenthood provides millions of women with cancer screenings, contraception, and STI testing. If Congress defunded Planned Parenthood, clinics could be forced to shut down. Women, especially low-income women in underserved areas, would have to go without care. _Unfortunately, as long as Republicans control both houses of Congress and the White House, they will continue to vilify Planned Parenthood._ Our only option is to keep making our voice heard. [_**We can 't take our eyes off the goal for a second. Vow to protect Planned Parenthood and speak out against attempts to defund it.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e26/622b7b5b/51c4119/307bdd88/3507139711/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e170805") Thank you, Debbie |

name: Tom Perez
subject: re: double-digit premium increases (add your name)
body:
If you can't repeal it, sabotage it.
---
**Trump is doing everything he can to sabotage the Affordable Care Act. Add your name to say you're committed to stopping him:

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-Health-Care-Sabotage1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170811_tp_nd&source=em_20170811_tp_nd")**

Derek --

Making sure Americans can get the health care they need shouldn't be a partisan issue. No one who works hard should be forced to choose between putting food on the table or paying a medical bill, no matter where you live or who you voted for.

But the sad reality is that one political party believes that health care is a right, and the other does not. And we're seeing Republicans' callousness on full display.

Donald Trump openly threatened to sabotage the Affordable Care Act -- and it's working. A new report yesterday revealed that Trump's recklessness on health care could lead to double-digit premium increases next year.

And even though Republicans still haven't managed to come up with a health care plan of their own, they're still obsessed with repealing Obamacare. Earlier today, a group of Republican congressmen from the extreme House Freedom Caucus filed a petition calling for total repeal of the Affordable Care Act with no replacement.

We've fended off the GOP's attacks on our health care because we banded together. But they won't give up -- so neither can we.

**Millions of Americans are counting on Democrats like us to protect affordable health care. I know we won't let them down, Derek. Add your name if you're committed to protecting our care no matter the cost:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-Health-Care-Sabotage1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170811_tp_nd&source=em_20170811_tp_nd")**
Thanks,

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. name: Andrew Gillum
subject: unapologetically progressive
body:
#
Team --
Yesterday, I spoke with many of you at Netroots Nation about the kind of campaign we're running. There are ideas about what our Democratic nominee for Florida Governor is supposed to talk about and sound like, and where they're supposed to come from. Well, I'm not using that playbook.
In fact, we're running a different kind of campaign from everyone else -- unapologetically progressive ideas, backed up by my record of service. But I'm hoping that you'll agree with me in this idea that maybe, just maybe, we need someone different.
[**If you didn 't get to see me at Netroots yesterday, watch our video now, and then if you feel inspired to do so, chip in whatever you can afford to fund this movement:**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32d6/9e00690/4ca97400/24036ef0/133094492/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017-nn17?refcode=em170811-vid")
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32d6/9e00690/4ca97400/24036ef0/133094492/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017-nn17?refcode=em170811-vid")
Like I said yesterday, what my grandmother taught me is that this isn't just about me. We are all in this together, and we have to work to make sure we ALL do well. We have to bring it home for every single Floridian, not just the well-heeled and the well-connected. And when we win this campaign for Governor of Florida, that's what we're going to accomplish together.
[**But I can 't do it alone -- I'm going to need your help. Will you watch our video, then chip in $3, $5, $10, $20, or whatever you can spare to make this victory possible?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb32d6/9e00690/4ca97400/24036ef0/133094492/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-august2017-nn17?refcode=em170811-vid")
Thank you for everything.
Bring it home,
Andrew

**_Indiana is On The Map_**
I can't say this for certain, but I'm pretty sure there is no other state in the nation that can lay claim to having hosted the Vice President of the United States and three Cabinet members in just the past week. Environmental Protection Agency Administrator **Scott Pruitt** , Secretary of Housing and Urban Development **Dr. Ben Carson** , Secretary of Agriculture **Sonny Perdue** and Vice President **Mike Pence** all made visits in the past week. Secretary Pruitt, as we mentioned in the last Party Line, made stops in Central Indiana with Governor **Eric Holcomb** , Secretary Carson visited East Chicago, Secretary Perdue toured the Indiana State Fair and discussed agriculture with a roundtable of local leaders and Vice President Pence keynoted a luncheon benefiting the Ten Point Coalition and attended the official unveiling of his gubernatorial portrait. These visits are a true testament to the importance our new federal partners put in the Indiana blueprint for prosperity and success. They know the answer key is found in the Indiana Story and they'll be coming again and again to learn more and more about how we did it and how other states - and the federal government - can do it, too!
\- Kyle

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer, Governor Holcomb and members of the 2017 Indiana Republican Party Summer Intern Class at the Young Professionals for Team Holcomb event that they planned and organized as part of their internship.)

clean_body: | | | | | | Indiana is On The Map I can't say this for certain, but I'm pretty sure there is no other state in the nation that can lay claim to having hosted the Vice President of the United States and three Cabinet members in just the past week. Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Scott Pruitt , Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Dr. Ben Carson , Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue and Vice President Mike Pence all made visits in the past week. Secretary Pruitt, as we mentioned in the last Party Line, made stops in Central Indiana with Governor Eric Holcomb , Secretary Carson visited East Chicago, Secretary Perdue toured the Indiana State Fair and discussed agriculture with a roundtable of local leaders and Vice President Pence keynoted a luncheon benefiting the Ten Point Coalition and attended the official unveiling of his gubernatorial portrait. These visits are a true testament to the importance our new federal partners put in the Indiana blueprint for prosperity and success. They know the answer key is found in the Indiana Story and they'll be coming again and again to learn more and more about how we did it and how other states - and the federal government - can do it, too! \- Kyle (Photo: Chairman Hupfer, Governor Holcomb and members of the 2017 Indiana Republican Party Summer Intern Class at the Young Professionals for Team Holcomb event that they planned and organized as part of their internship.) | | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | | With the Indiana State Fair well underway, Governor Holcomb spent some quality time with Secretary of Agriculture Sonny Perdue this week. The Governor also spent some time saying hello to fellow Hoosiers at the Statehouse Market and helping to of the USS Indianapolis along with some of America's fine sailors. | | | Eric Holcomb Day at the Indiana State Fair is TOMORROW Tomorrow, Saturday August 12, is Eric Holcomb Day at the Indiana Republican Party's State Fair Booth. We'll be celebrating the proven leadership of Indiana's 51st Governor all day long, but make sure you visit us at 5:30pm when the man of the day will be there himself! **NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, August 11th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| **Dear Derek,**
**It’s hard to believe that September is right around the corner.**
**We’ve been busy at the NH Republican State Committee this summer...from managing special elections to running our iGOP Intern Program, and regular meetings of our standing committees on By-Laws and Platform. It is truly inspiring to see the kind of dedicated stalwart Republicans we have helping to lead our party. Because to be successful, we all need to play a part!**
**Just recently I attended a meeting of the By-Laws Committee on a hot summer evening in Concord. These committee members made a commitment and deliberate effort to spend their valuable time working to put together By-Law improvements. They are working on these improvements NOW so that all State Committee members can consider the changes prior to our January 27, 2018 Annual Meeting.**
**This past week I attended a packed house and very successful fundraiser for Sen. Ruth Ward in Hillsborough, stopped by the Manchester Republican Committee’s summer fundraiser, talked with members of the Pelham Republican Committee at their monthly meeting, and met with three candidates who are running in special elections.**
**I am pleased to say that everywhere I go, enthusiasm remains high. Republicans are getting things done. Republicans have made the necessary course correction and we are headed to economic prosperity thanks to one of the most popular Governors in the country...and[Governor Sununu’s numbers continue to rise according to the most recent poll.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyEOrLuxQY7Dv06piz2w2h8hcxRA8tXMEETZeqiNJxpE-2Flmfxq6pRuBYYupTCdvQYyGXJ7Vtk1h5RsuYOF1ZvnAsvV1ADHalzbfFZsm3pZhSi8iWXlra0JI-2BwmeTCqy6kAw-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MUDv4tTBBHhzjx9jGbvy3Zayqjva4gfOH96j4NFJDBhYEvnxVuX6NW5uVtDx8zp4TKxnsMpvYGJlGVdptUdH7rqzQoDQppdpClawa-2BhQe-2BMaQ0aAADGNv8LGnh-2B2CZg8j-2Ft-2FanKiyvRjnYxgYcyA3b-2FMIitK7Ljtevbp1mbIPp30ooAs3M-2FdwiLIWnkJrPl-2BXcKdBYIhKqjZrDtrpA00LC4DCfuCqj-2FYRP2hfXuzzbOVWxcVbpTE2dCO1ermgoDLhwo6yX-2F95AfqIm15hrvAhGw-3D-3D)**
**We are also working on our RED Summit. This summit will include Republican leaders, activists, and Republican State Committee members from across the state. We will move into 2018 with a solid plan of action to grow our majorities at a state level and take back _our_ Congressional seats. Please mark your calendar and plan to attend on September 30th.**
**Sincerely,**
**_Jeanie_**

clean_body: | | | NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, August 11th | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, It’s hard to believe that September is right around the corner. We’ve been busy at the NH Republican State Committee this summer...from managing special elections to running our iGOP Intern Program, and regular meetings of our standing committees on By-Laws and Platform. It is truly inspiring to see the kind of dedicated stalwart Republicans we have helping to lead our party. Because to be successful, we all need to play a part! Just recently I attended a meeting of the By-Laws Committee on a hot summer evening in Concord. These committee members made a commitment and deliberate effort to spend their valuable time working to put together By-Law improvements. They are working on these improvements NOW so that all State Committee members can consider the changes prior to our January 27, 2018 Annual Meeting. This past week I attended a packed house and very successful fundraiser for Sen. Ruth Ward in Hillsborough, stopped by the Manchester Republican Committee’s summer fundraiser, talked with members of the Pelham Republican Committee at their monthly meeting, and met with three candidates who are running in special elections. I am pleased to say that everywhere I go, enthusiasm remains high. Republicans are getting things done. Republicans have made the necessary course correction and we are headed to economic prosperity thanks to one of the most popular Governors in the country...and We are also working on our RED Summit. This summit will include Republican leaders, activists, and Republican State Committee members from across the state. We will move into 2018 with a solid plan of action to grow our majorities at a state level and take back our Congressional seats. Please mark your calendar and plan to attend on September 30th.
